title: Neem hair oil
subtitle: A scalp treatment oil with neem oil, coconut oil, and rosemary to support scalp health
excerpt: Neem oil has a strong, distinctive smell and documented activity against scalp conditions caused by specific fungi. Diluted in coconut oil with rosemary essential oil, it becomes a scalp treatment oil applied before shampooing. The smell rinses out with shampoo.
sourceNotes: Neem oil (Azadirachta indica) properties: Bhatt et al., Journal of Pharmacy and Pharmacology (2000). Neem oil in scalp care: Draelos, Cosmetic Dermatology (2010). Note: neem oil should not be applied undiluted; recommended cosmetic-use dilution is 2 to 5% in a carrier oil. This blend uses neem at approximately 10% as a scalp treatment left on briefly before washing. Rosemary EO for scalp: Panahi et al., SKINmed (2015).

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Stable preparation; make a full batch and store for up to 6 months.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 6 months in a cool dark place. Neem oil oxidises over time; discard if the oil smells sharply rancid rather than the usual neem scent, or if it becomes noticeably darker.

glossaryTerms.definition: A water-free preparation. This oil blend contains no water and needs no preservative.

content.text: Neem oil is a cold-pressed oil from the seeds of the neem tree (Azadirachta indica). It has a strong, earthy smell described variously as sulphurous, garlic-like, or similar to peanut butter. The smell is the main barrier to use; diluted in coconut oil and rinsed out with shampoo, it is barely noticeable in the dry hair. This
blend uses neem at 10% as a pre-shampoo scalp treatment. Rosemary essential oil contributes a fresh scent that partially masks the neem.

items.prepNote: 80% of blend; fractionated coconut oil stays liquid in cold weather
10% of blend; cold-pressed; strong smell is normal
10% of blend; for slip and scalp coating

content.text: If using unfractionated coconut oil that is solid at room temperature, warm gently until just melted. Combine all oils and the rosemary essential oil in a clean bottle. Swirl to mix. Label with the date and contents.

Apply 10 to 15 drops directly to the scalp, not the hair length. Massage in with fingertips and leave for 20 to 30 minutes before shampooing. Shampoo twice to remove the oil fully. Use once or twice a week as a scalp treatment.
Apply a small amount to the inner forearm and wait 24 to 48 hours before using on the scalp. This is standard practice for any new preparation.

Keep in a cool dark place. Keeps 6 months. Neem oil has a shorter shelf life than most carrier oils because it is high in unsaturated fatty acids that oxidise faster. Discard if the blend smells sharply rancid rather than the normal neem odour.
